name: Vaupes (Colombia)

colors.symbolism: peace and the rivers that thread through the rainforest
the dense Amazonian rainforest covering the department

description: A field divided horizontally white over green, with a stylised dark green branch or vine motif over the lower stripe.
history: Vaupes was elevated from a commissary to a department of Colombia in 1991. Its flag uses white and green to represent the rivers and the rainforest, charged with a stylised branch evoking the Amazonian flora.

features.symbolism: the abundant Amazonian flora native to the department
